req.title: Hr-Admin deletes department
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven department deletion by an hr-admin. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid hr-admin deletes an existing department, expects 200/201 + audit record; (2) NEG – unauthenticated user attempts deletion, expects 401; (3) NEG/DT – forbidden role (employee) attempts deletion, expects 403; (4) BVA – department name at maximum allowed boundary length is deleted successfully; (5) STC – system transitions from department-exists state through deletion to department-absent state with audit trail. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create/clean up data and is idempotent through unique identifiers per run.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_department_success
preconditions: A department with a known ID exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /departments
Locate the target department row
Click the Delete action for the department
Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Capture the HTTP response status and body
Query the audit log for a deletion record
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity ID
Department no longer appears in the /departments listing
Audit record exists for the deletion event
trace: R-WEB-007: positive path – hr-admin deletes department
R-WEB-007: audit record written on success
R-WEB-007: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_deletes_department_success(hr_admin_session, create_department, audit_log_client):
    dept = create_department(name="Dept-EP-" + <unique_id>())
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    page = hr_admin_session.navigate("/departments")
    <click_delete_for_entity>(page, dept_id)
    <confirm_dialog>(page)

    response = hr_admin_session.last_response()
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert str(dept_id) in response.text

    listing = hr_admin_session.get_json("/departments")
    ids = [d["id"] for d in listing]
    assert dept_id not in ids

    audit = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=dept_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "hr-admin"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_department
preconditions: No active session / user is unauthenticated
A department with a known ID exists
actions: Send DELETE request to /departments/{id} without an auth token
Capture the HTTP response status
expected: Department still exists in the system
No audit record written for this attempt
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_department(anon_api_client, create_department, audit_log_client):
    dept = create_department(name="Dept-Unauth-" + <unique_id>())
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    response = anon_api_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 401

    audit = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=dept_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None

    <cleanup_department>(dept_id)

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_department
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: employee (not hr-admin)
A department with a known ID exists
actions: Authenticate as an employee-role user
Send DELETE request to /departments/{id} with employee auth token
Capture the HTTP response status
expected: Department still exists in the system
No audit record written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-007: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_department(employee_api_client, create_department, audit_log_client):
    dept = create_department(name="Dept-Forbidden-" + <unique_id>())
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    response = employee_api_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 403

    audit = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=dept_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None

    <cleanup_department>(dept_id)

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_department_with_max_length_name
preconditions: A department whose name is exactly at the maximum allowed character length exists
actions: Create a department with a name of exactly MAX_DEPT_NAME_LEN characters
Navigate to /departments
Locate and delete the max-length-name department
Confirm deletion
Capture response status and body
Query audit log
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity ID
Department no longer appears in the /departments listing
Audit record exists for the deletion event
trace: R-WEB-007: boundary – department name at MAX length is still deletable
R-WEB-007: mandatory field validation must not reject a name at the boundary
tests.executable:
MAX_DEPT_NAME_LEN = <get_config>("department.name.max_length")

def test_hr_admin_deletes_department_with_max_length_name(hr_admin_session, create_department, audit_log_client):
    max_name = "D" * MAX_DEPT_NAME_LEN
    dept = create_department(name=max_name)
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    page = hr_admin_session.navigate("/departments")
    <click_delete_for_entity>(page, dept_id)
    <confirm_dialog>(page)

    response = hr_admin_session.last_response()
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert str(dept_id) in response.text

    listing = hr_admin_session.get_json("/departments")
    assert dept_id not in [d["id"] for d in listing]

    audit = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=dept_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None

tests.name: test_department_state_transitions_on_delete
actions: Verify department is in ACTIVE state via GET /departments/{id}
Submit delete request via UI form
Confirm deletion dialog
Verify department state transitions to DELETED (or is absent)
Verify audit log records the ACTIVE -> DELETED transition
expected: Before deletion: GET /departments/{id} returns 200 with status ACTIVE
After deletion: GET /departments/{id} returns 404 or status DELETED
Audit record captures previous state ACTIVE and new state DELETED
No further mutations possible on the deleted department (idempotency guard)
trace: R-WEB-007: state transition ACTIVE -> DELETED on successful delete
R-WEB-007: audit record written capturing state change
tests.executable:
def test_department_state_transitions_on_delete(hr_admin_session, create_department, audit_log_client, admin_api_client):
    dept = create_department(name="Dept-STC-" + <unique_id>())
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    pre = admin_api_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"

    page = hr_admin_session.navigate("/departments")
    <click_delete_for_entity>(page, dept_id)
    <confirm_dialog>(page)

    post = admin_api_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert post.status_code in (404, 200)
    if post.status_code == 200:
        assert post.json()["status"] == "DELETED"

    audit = audit_log_client.find_latest(entity_id=dept_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit.get("previous_state") == "ACTIVE"
